title: Adopting a shared protocol library: file the gap, then annotate the mitigation
description: When swapping a hand-maintained subsystem for a shared library, never absorb a capability gap silently. For each gap: open an upstream issue with the call site and a suggested API shape, implement the mitigation, and comment the mitigation at the call site naming the upstream request. Phase12's Thunder server swap produced five (hivellm/thunder#1-#5): Value::Bytes(Vec<u8>) killing the zero-copy GET/SET path, no per-listener max_connections, no per-command metrics hook, Principal carrying only a name, and ListenerHandle::stop(self) blocking a shared observer. All five were fixed upstream within a day (thunder-rpc 0.1.2/0.2.0) and every mitigation was reverted before release. Write the issue first, then the mitigation - the issue is what gets the workaround deleted.
whenToUse: Any adoption of a shared/external library that replaces first-party code with its own opinions — protocol crates, runtimes, storage engines.
whenNotToUse: Cosmetic API differences with no behavioral or performance consequence; noise upstream costs more than it returns.
